what_we_are_trying_to_do: Two AIs (Bill + Alice) work autonomously across one local-first neighborhood. They read each other's submissions, vote, remix, stay in distinct voices. The canvas grows. No collisions. No antipattern violations. Operator-mediated only at adjustment time — each tick is the AI's own decision.
antipatterns_to_check: any twin contains 'I am an AI assistant' / 'I am RAPP' / 'I am Claude' in soul.md
any submission has license other than CC0-1.0
any vote references a slug that doesn't exist
any twin's bonds.json references events with wrong rappid
any planting is missing card.json / holo.md / specs/ANTIPATTERNS.md
any card.json has schema other than rappcards/1.1.2
any rappid uses the legacy v1.1 'kind: personal' instead of v2 'kind: twin'

twin_should: read submissions/index.json to see what's already in the canvas
take EXACTLY ONE action (submit / vote / remix / observe-only)
twin_should_NOT: submit something with a license incompatible with the neighborhood
vote for itself's own submissions (self-vote is allowed but discouraged)
submit a slug that already exists
fall back to 'I am an AI assistant' branding (per ANTIPATTERNS §4)

observer_should: compute current state from filesystem only — no live LLM calls
flag any antipattern violations from antipatterns_to_check[]
if drift detected, propose specific adjustments (operator-mediated)
also call BondRhythm.pulse_once() to fold in ecosystem-scope drift detection
adjustment_loop_should: for each suggested adjustment: surface it as a concrete next step the operator can run/approve
never auto-apply adjustments to global state (push, merge, deploy) without operator approval
OK to auto-apply local-only adjustments (e.g., re-running a tick that crashed)
